name: Kalihi-Palama
desc: Gritty working-class neighborhood with incredible Filipino food — authentic local culture, but highest crime in metro Honolulu.
insider: Helena's Hawaiian Food won the James Beard Award and serves the best pipikaula (dried beef) and lau lau on the island — cash only
Kalihi has the most authentic Filipino food outside Manila — Thelma's for lumpia, Max's for fried chicken, and Elena's for oxtail
Stick to main roads after dark and be aware of your surroundings — HPD crime stats rank Kalihi-Palama among the highest in Honolulu
Bishop Museum is technically in Kalihi and is the best museum in all of Hawaii — the Hawaiian Hall alone is worth the $26 admission
The Mayor Wright Housing complex has had persistent crime issues — the area around it is best avoided at night
Gulick Avenue and Dillingham Blvd have incredible hole-in-the-wall eateries — cheap, authentic, and packed with locals at lunch
Kalihi Transit Center is a major bus hub — you can get almost anywhere on the island from here without a transfer
The neighborhood has deep Pacific Islander roots — Samoan, Tongan, Marshallese, and Filipino communities thrive side by side
Property is among the most affordable in metro Honolulu — you can find homes under $700K close to downtown
Kalihi Waena Elementary hosts a fantastic multicultural fair annually — food from a dozen Pacific Island nations in one place
